message.content: The battlefield stretched like a festering wound across the shattered plain, its churned earth glutted with the swollen husks of the fallen. Corpses lay in grotesque tableaux—limbs sheared away by cannon fire, ribs splayed open like broken cages exposing the pale gleam of bone gnawed by opportunistic crows and rats. A cloying miasma hung heavy in the air, mingling the coppery tang of congealed blood with the sickly-sweet rot of flesh peeling back from muscle, while flies droned in thick, buzzing clouds over eyes long glazed milky and sightless. The wind carried faint, wet squelches from bloating bellies split by gas, and beneath it all, a pervasive silence broken only by the distant, ravenous skitter of unseen scavengers claiming their due. Here, death was no swift mercy, but a lingering thief, stripping valor to base meat under an indifferent sky.
